heading: Committee on Mining and Mineral Resources Research
label: 1229 Committee on Mining and Mineral Resources Research
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Mining and Mineral Resources Institutes Act, and not as part of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 which comprises this chapter.

children.text: the Assistant Secretary of the Interior responsible for minerals and mining research, or his delegate;
the Director, United States Bureau of Mines, or his delegate;
the Director, United States Geological Survey, or his delegate;
the Director of the National Science Foundation, or his delegate;
the President, National Academy of Sciences, or his delegate;
the President, National Academy of Engineering, or his delegate; and

not more than 7 other persons who are knowledgeable in the fields of mining and mineral resources research, including two university administrators involved in the conduct of programs authorized by this subchapter, 3 representatives from the mining industry, a working miner, and a representative from the conservation community. In making these 7 appointments, the Secretary shall consult with interested groups.

children.heading: Consultation and recommendations
children.label: b Consultation and recommendations
children.text: The Committee shall consult with, and make recommendations to, the Secretary on all matters relating to mining and mineral resources research and the determinations that are required to be made under this subchapter. The Secretary shall consult with, and consider recommendations of, such Committee in such matters.

children.heading: Compensation, travel, subsistence and related expenses
children.label: c Compensation, travel, subsistence and related expenses
children.text: Committee members, other than officers or employees of Federal, State, or local governments, shall be, for each day (including traveltime) during which they are performing Committee business, paid at a rate fixed by the Secretary but not excess of the daily equivalent of the maximum rate of pay for grade GS–18 of the General Schedule under , and shall be fully reimbursed for travel, subsistence, and related expenses.1section 5332 of title 5So in original. Probably should be followed by “in”.1

children.heading: Chairmanship of Committee
children.label: d Chairmanship of Committee
children.text: The Committee shall be jointly chaired by the Assistant Secretary of the Interior responsible for minerals and mining and a person to be elected by the Committee from among the members referred to in paragraphs (5), (6), and (7) of subsection (a) of this section.

children.heading: National plan for research
children.label: e National plan for research
children.text: The Committee shall develop a national plan for research in mining and mineral resources, considering ongoing efforts in the universities, the Federal Government, and the private sector, and shall formulate and recommend a program to implement the plan utilizing resources provided for under this subchapter. The Committee shall submit such plan to the Secretary, the President, and the Congress on or before , and shall submit an annual update of such plan by January 15 of each calendar year.March 1, 1986

children.heading: Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: f Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: shall not apply to the Committee.Section 1009 of title 5
